title: Perception-action loop driver with budgets and failure handling
description: The orchestrator. Per turn: stabilize → diff → build prompt → decide → validate (≤3 retries) → act → persist. Enforces per-turn wall-clock budget (default 30s), per-session turn budget (default 50), and per-session wall-clock budget (default 15min). Implements all PRD §15 failure modes: stability budget expired, invalid action retries, malformed model output retries, three-failed-turns -> harness_error: agent_stuck, runtime app errors don't end session, VM connection lost ends session preserving log, binding-not-initialized exit, budget exhaustion clean end. Voluntary end via done(reason). PRD §8, §15.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] One full turn completes against a sample app under all default policies
- [ ] Per-turn and per-session budgets terminate sessions cleanly
- [ ] Three consecutive failed turns ends with harness_error: agent_stuck
- [ ] Lost VM connection ends with harness_error: connection_lost and preserves the trajectory
- [ ] done(reason) ends the session and writes a final summary

title: Action validator against current semantics tree
description: Given a candidate action and the current observation, validate: target node ID exists, node is enabled and visible, action type is allowed for that node's role/available-actions list, payload is well-formed (e.g. enter_text has a string, scroll has direction+distance). On rejection, return a structured failure with the validation error message. Driver retries the same observation up to 3 times before counting a failed turn. PRD §8 step 5, §15.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Returns ok | reject(reason) for any candidate action
- [ ] Rejects unknown node IDs, disabled nodes, off-screen nodes
- [ ] Rejects action types not present in the node's available-actions list
- [ ] Validates payload shape per action kind
- [ ] Rejection messages are designed to be model-readable

title: Model provider abstraction with constrained output (MLX, Anthropic, OpenAI)
description: ModelProvider interface plus three v1 implementations: MLX OpenAI-compatible (primary, targeting Qwen3 35B-A3B at 8-bit), Anthropic, OpenAI. Every model call enforces a JSON schema constraining output to {action, summary_update, rationale?, wait_strategy?}. Outputs that don't match are rejected and re-prompted once before counting as a failed turn. Ships sensible per-tier defaults (temperature, validation retries, observation budgets). PRD §14.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] ModelProvider interface defined with completion + schema-constrained variants
- [ ] MLX OpenAI-compatible client works against a local MLX endpoint
- [ ] Anthropic and OpenAI clients work against their respective APIs
- [ ] Schema-constrained output rejects non-conforming completions and retries once
- [ ] Per-tier defaults documented and applied based on model identifier

title: Observation assembly & per-turn structural diff
description: Harness module that pulls an observation bundle (via getStableObservation), normalizes it, and computes the structural diff against the previous turn's observation: route changes (push/pop/replace), nodes added (with full descriptions), nodes removed (by id), and nodes changed (id + field-level diff). Diff is harness-authored and included verbatim in the next prompt. PRD §8 step 2, §9.7, §11.2.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Observation pulled in a single VM call returns a typed bundle
- [ ] Structural diff has four lists: routeChanges, nodesAdded, nodesRemoved, nodesChanged
- [ ] Diff is stable / order-independent for unchanged regions
- [ ] First-turn diff treats prior observation as empty without crashing

title: Harness scaffolding & VM service connection
description: exploration_agent CLI shell that connects to a running app's VM service URI, locates the ExplorationBinding's extension namespace, performs the handshake (binding ping), and surfaces a clear actionable error if the binding is missing or wrong version. Provides the connection abstraction every later harness story uses. PRD §6.2.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] CLI accepts --vm-uri and connects to a running app
- [ ] Detects ExplorationBinding presence via the handshake extension
- [ ] Fails with a setup-pointing error if the binding is absent
- [ ] Provides a typed VmServiceClient abstraction reused by later stories

title: Stable observation primitive (getStableObservation + policies)
description: Binding extension getStableObservation(policy) that waits for the policy's stability condition (or its bounded timeout) and then captures and returns: semantics tree, route stack, runtime errors since cursor, and a stability metadata block (policy used, termination reason, wall-clock duration, what was still busy). Implements three policies: action-relative (default), quiet-frame, bounded-stability. PRD §7, §9.4.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] action-relative policy terminates on route or semantics change OR budget (default 800ms)
- [ ] quiet-frame policy terminates after N consecutive idle frames (default N=2)
- [ ] bounded-stability policy terminates on quiet-frame OR budget (default 1500ms)
- [ ] Returned stability block reports termination reason, duration, and any still-busy work
- [ ] Single VM extension call returns the full observation bundle

title: Action executor for all v1 action kinds
description: Implement the in-binding executor for: tap, long_press, enter_text, scroll, scroll_until_visible, gesture (discrete kinds — pinch, swipe, etc.), system_back, wait (rejected if >5s), and inspect_widget. Resolve semantic node IDs to live hit-test targets at execution time. Expose via 'ext.exploration.executeAction'. inspect_widget returns the widget tree for the requested node — full-turn cost per PRD §17 item 2 lean. PRD §10.1, §10.2.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] All v1 actions execute against a live ExplorationBinding-instrumented app
- [ ] Actions target semantic node IDs, not coordinates
- [ ] enter_text correctly focuses the target before typing
- [ ] scroll_until_visible terminates with a clear error if target is unreachable
- [ ] inspect_widget returns the widget subtree for the target
- [ ] wait rejects values >5s

title: Semantics tree capture with stable IDs and configurable interactives
description: Walk the semantics tree, assign session-stable numeric identifiers, filter off-screen / fully-obscured / excludeSemantics nodes, and emit role / label / state-flags / available-actions per node. Provide a Marionette-style 'isInteractiveWidget' configuration hook so apps with custom design systems can promote their custom controls into the captured tree. Expose via 'ext.exploration.getSemanticsTree'. PRD §9.1, §10.2.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Semantics tree is serialized as compact node records (id, role, label, state, actions)
- [ ] Off-screen / excluded / obscured nodes are omitted by default
- [ ] Stable IDs persist across observations within a session
- [ ] Configurable hook surfaces app-defined custom interactives
- [ ] getSemanticsTree extension returns the captured tree

title: Frame stability signal collection in the binding
description: Hook SchedulerBinding to track transient callbacks (animations), persistent callbacks, pending microtasks, and frame commit/skip events. Maintain bounded counters and timestamps suitable for stability decisions. Expose via 'ext.exploration.getFrameState'. Foundation for the stable-observation primitive (§7, §8). PRD §6.1.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Counts of in-flight transient and persistent callbacks are observable
- [ ] Pending microtask presence is observable
- [ ] Frame commit / skip events are timestamped and exposed
- [ ] getFrameState VM extension returns a snapshot suitable for stability evaluation

title: ExplorationBinding skeleton, lifecycle, and conflict detection
description: Core ExplorationBinding extends WidgetsFlutterBinding. Provides ExplorationBinding.ensureInitialized() called from main(), kDebugMode/profile-mode gate, hard-error if another custom binding (e.g. IntegrationTestWidgetsFlutterBinding) is already installed, and the empty VM service extension namespace under which all later extensions register. Also defines the VM service extension naming convention/contract used by harness-side stories. PRD §6.1.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] ExplorationBinding installs as the WidgetsBinding when initialized in main()
- [ ] Throws a clear error if another custom binding has already been installed
- [ ] Becomes a no-op (or refuses to init) outside debug/profile modes
- [ ] Registers the 'ext.exploration.*' namespace and a ping extension for harness handshake
- [ ] VM service extension naming/contract is documented

title: Repo & package scaffolding for exploration_flutter and exploration_agent
description: Lay down the two Dart packages this project ships: 'exploration_flutter' (Flutter package with the in-app binding) and 'exploration_agent' (Dart CLI / library implementing the harness). Includes pubspecs, analysis_options.yaml, lints, baseline CI (analyze + test), and a workspace layout that lets both packages co-evolve. Blocks every downstream story.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Two-package monorepo layout with shared lints
- [ ] Both packages compile, pass analyze, and run an empty test suite
- [ ] CI baseline runs analyze + test on PRs

title: Decide route-stack fallback for Navigator 2.0 declarative apps
description: The PRD's route-stack abstraction (§9.2) assumes a Navigator 1.0 stack readable as a list. For apps using Navigator 2.0 declaratively (Router + RouterDelegate), there is no stack to read directly — only the active Router configuration. We need a validated approach for deriving a meaningful route representation from a Nav 2.0 app before E5 (route stack capture) can ship. Open question from PRD §17 item 1.
design:
Approach options:
1. Best-effort serialization of the active Router/RouteInformationProvider configuration as the 'top route'; treat the stack as length-1 unless multiple Pages are active.
2. Walk the underlying Pages list from the Navigator widget actually mounted by the RouterDelegate (works for go_router, Beamer, auto_route which all use Pages under the hood).
3. Provide a configuration hook so the app supplies its own RouteAdapter when defaults are insufficient.

Validate against at least one real go_router app and one custom RouterDelegate app before locking in the contract.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Route-stack contract documented and approved for both Nav1 and Nav2 paths
- [ ] Validation completed against ≥1 go_router-based sample app
- [ ] Decision recorded in PRD addendum or ADR

title: CLI surface and AGENTS.md template
description: exploration_agent CLI with --goal, --vm-uri, --model, --output (trajectory path), --policy (initial), and per-tier sensible defaults. Includes a minimal AGENTS.md template the user can copy and fill in. Wraps E15's loop driver. PRD §16 'Ships in v1'.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] CLI parses required and optional flags with helpful --help output
- [ ] Defaults vary by model identifier per E13's tier table
- [ ] AGENTS.md template is shipped under exploration_agent/templates and referenced in --help
- [ ] End-to-end: 'exploration_agent --goal X --vm-uri Y --model Z' runs a session against a sample app

title: Trajectory writer (JSONL) with session metadata and final record
description: JSONL writer for the trajectory log: header with session metadata (goal, AGENTS.md hash, build identifier, model identifier, harness version), one record per turn (observation, stability metadata, proposed action, validation result, executed action, diff, summary update), and a final record (outcome: done | budget_exhausted | harness_error, final summary). PRD §12.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] JSONL file contains a typed header line followed by per-turn records
- [ ] Per-turn record schema matches PRD §12
- [ ] Final record is always written, even on harness_error
- [ ] Files are flushed per turn so a crashed session preserves progress

title: Memory artifacts and prompt construction
description: Implements the two memory artifacts and assembles the per-turn prompt. Running summary buffer is model-authored, soft 500-token budget, hard 1000-token cap (rejects updates over cap, prompts model to compress). Last N actions buffer (3-5 verbatim with one-line outcomes). Prompt assembler combines: AGENTS.md (static prior) + goal + running summary + last N actions + current observation + diff + stability metadata. PRD §8 step 3, §11.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Running summary enforces soft and hard token budgets
- [ ] Last-N actions buffer trims to a fixed window
- [ ] Prompt assembler produces a single string/structured payload conforming to provider input
- [ ] AGENTS.md content is included verbatim
- [ ] Diff and stability metadata are included verbatim

title: Diagnostics for poor-semantics apps
description: At connect / first observation, scan the widget tree for interactable-looking widgets (GestureDetector, InkWell, custom buttons) that lack semantics annotations and aren't promoted via the isInteractiveWidget hook. Surface a clear diagnostic to the harness so the user knows the agent is operating partially blind. PRD §10.2, §18 risk #3.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Diagnostic identifies common interactable widgets without semantics
- [ ] Surfaces results to the harness on connect via a dedicated extension
- [ ] Suggests the isInteractiveWidget hook in the diagnostic message
- [ ] Sample-app fixture demonstrates a hit and a clean run

title: Runtime error ring buffer with chaining strategy
description: Bounded ring buffer (default 50 entries) capturing unhandled exceptions, framework asserts, and render errors emitted since the previous turn. Hooks FlutterError.onError and PlatformDispatcher.instance.onError. Each entry carries message, first 5 stack frames, and wall-clock offset. Implements the error-handler chaining strategy chosen in D2. Expose via 'ext.exploration.getRecentErrors' (delta since cursor). PRD §6.1, §9.3 — gated by D2 design decision.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] FlutterError.onError and PlatformDispatcher onError are hooked
- [ ] Pre-existing app handlers are chained per D2
- [ ] Ring buffer is bounded (default 50, configurable)
- [ ] Each entry contains message + first 5 stack frames + offset
- [ ] getRecentErrors extension supports a 'since' cursor for per-turn delta

title: Route stack capture (Navigator 1 + best-effort Navigator 2)
description: Capture the current navigator stack, top to bottom, as a list of route names plus exposed RouteSettings.arguments. Implement Navigator 1.0 reading directly. Implement the Navigator 2.0 fallback derived from the active Router configuration as decided in D1. Expose via 'ext.exploration.getRouteStack'. PRD §9.2 — gated by D1 design decision.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Returns a top-to-bottom list of routes for Navigator 1.0 apps
- [ ] Implements the Nav 2.0 fallback strategy chosen in D1
- [ ] Includes RouteSettings.arguments where available
- [ ] Validated against ≥1 Nav 1.0 app and ≥1 Nav 2.0 / go_router app

title: Decide FlutterError.onError chaining strategy
description: ExplorationBinding registers with FlutterError.onError and PlatformDispatcher.instance.onError to populate its runtime-error ring buffer (PRD §6.1, §9.3, §17 item 3). Apps that already install custom error handlers (Sentry, Crashlytics, custom logging) will conflict. We need a documented chaining pattern so the binding does not silently swallow the host app's handler.
design:
Approach options:
1. Capture and chain: on init, snapshot the existing handler, install ours, and forward every event to the prior handler after recording. Mirrors how integration_test does it. Risk: re-entrancy if user code reinstalls after binding init.
2. Configuration hook: require users to explicitly opt their custom handler into the binding via ExplorationConfiguration.previousErrorHandler. More verbose, no silent surprises.
3. Hybrid: auto-chain by default, surface a diagnostic warning at connect time if a non-default handler was detected so users can confirm chaining is the right behavior.

Recommendation: option 3. Decide and document the official pattern before E6 ships.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Chaining pattern selected and documented
- [ ] ExplorationConfiguration API for error-handler chaining is specified
- [ ] Pattern verified by chaining behind a Sentry-like handler in the sample app

title: Flutter Exploration Agent
close_reason: Duplicate of lenny-px9 (created seconds later). Keeping lenny-px9 as the canonical Flutter Exploration Agent epic.

title: Flutter Exploration Agent
description: A Dart-based agent harness that drives a running Flutter app via a custom WidgetsBinding (Marionette-style), centering each agent turn on a stable observation. Goal-conditioned and open-ended: explore an app, reach a specific state, or characterize an unfamiliar app. Replaces test-framework primitives like pumpAndSettle with explicit stability policies grounded in observable frame-lifecycle signals. Runs against the real running app, not a test entrypoint. Spec: docs/flutter_exploration_agent_prd_v0.2.md.
design:
## Decomposition (PRD v0.2 → v1 stories)

### Decision beads (must resolve before downstream stories)
- lenny-ju0 (P1): Navigator 2.0 route-stack fallback design — blocks E5
- lenny-80r (P2): FlutterError.onError chaining strategy — blocks E6

### Stories
**Phase 0 — Scaffolding**
- lenny-px9.1 (P1): Repo & package scaffolding for exploration_flutter and exploration_agent

**Phase 1 — In-app binding (exploration_flutter)**
- lenny-px9.2 (P1): ExplorationBinding skeleton, lifecycle, conflict detection, ext namespace
- lenny-px9.3 (P1): Frame stability signal collection (SchedulerBinding hooks)
- lenny-px9.4 (P1): Semantics tree capture with stable IDs and configurable interactives
- lenny-px9.5 (P2): Route stack capture (Nav 1 + best-effort Nav 2) — blocked on D1
- lenny-px9.6 (P2): Runtime error ring buffer with chaining strategy — blocked on D2
- lenny-px9.7 (P1): Action executor for all v1 action kinds
- lenny-px9.8 (P1): Stable observation primitive (getStableObservation + 3 policies)
- lenny-px9.9 (P2): Diagnostics for poor-semantics apps

**Phase 2 — Harness (exploration_agent)**
- lenny-px9.10 (P1): Harness scaffolding & VM service connection
- lenny-px9.11 (P1): Observation assembly & per-turn structural diff
- lenny-px9.12 (P2): Memory artifacts and prompt construction
- lenny-px9.13 (P1): Model provider abstraction with constrained output (MLX, Anthropic, OpenAI)
- lenny-px9.14 (P1): Action validator against current semantics tree
- lenny-px9.15 (P1): Perception-action loop driver with budgets and failure handling
- lenny-px9.16 (P2): Trajectory writer (JSONL)
- lenny-px9.17 (P2): CLI surface & AGENTS.md template
- lenny-px9.18 (P3): CLI trajectory viewer

**Follow-ups (not v1-critical)**
- lenny-px9.19 (P3): Sample non-trivial Flutter app for dogfooding
- lenny-px9.20 (P3, deferred 30d): Diff compression for repeat visits

### Build order
L0: lenny-px9.1
L1: lenny-px9.2  (also: D1, D2, F1 unblocked in parallel)
L2: lenny-px9.3, lenny-px9.4, lenny-px9.10  (parallel; D1/D2 may resolve here)
L3: lenny-px9.5 (after D1), lenny-px9.6 (after D2), lenny-px9.7 (after .4), lenny-px9.9 (after .4), lenny-px9.13 (after .10), lenny-px9.16 (after .10)
L4: lenny-px9.8 (after .3,.4,.5,.6), lenny-px9.11 (after .10,.8), lenny-px9.14 (after .11)
L5: lenny-px9.12 (after .11)
L6: lenny-px9.15 (convergence: after .7,.11,.12,.13,.14,.16)
L7: lenny-px9.17 (after .15), lenny-px9.18 (after .16)

### Design decisions captured here (no separate bead needed)
- inspect_widget consumes a full turn (PRD §17 #2) — captured in E7; v1 lean per PRD, revisit in v2.
- 'wait for X to appear' action (PRD §17 #4): explicitly out of v1 scope; agent uses wait_strategy on next turn.
- Human-in-the-loop 'ask for help' (PRD §17 #6): explicitly out of v1.

### Open questions deferred (not blocking decomposition)
- PRD §17 #5 (diff compression) tracked as lenny-px9.20, deferred 30d.

### v2 / out-of-scope (per PRD §16 'Not in v1')
- Adversarial exploration (§13.3)
- Trajectory replay under integration_test (§13.4)
- Multi-device parallel sessions
- Web UI for trajectory browsing
- Trajectory-based fine-tuning pipelines (§13.5)
- Custom gestures beyond discrete kinds
- Demonstration recording (human driving while harness records)
- Hot-reload-aware sessions
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] User can install the binding, modify main(), run app under flutter run, and start a session in <10 minutes
- [ ] Qwen3 35B-A3B at 8-bit completes 'log in → settings → change setting → log out' on a representative non-trivial app in <20 turns ≥80% of attempts
- [ ] action-relative stability policy terminates within budget on ≥95% of post-action turns without missing observable changes
- [ ] Single open-ended exploration session yields a useful structural map of an unfamiliar app
- [ ] 30-minute session produces a trajectory <50MB reviewable in the CLI viewer in one sitting

title: Sample non-trivial Flutter app for dogfooding & success criteria
description: PRD §19 success criteria reference 'a representative non-trivial app' (login → settings → change setting → logout) and 'a 30-minute exploration session against a real running app'. Without a controlled sample app we cannot evaluate the success metrics. Either build one in this repo under examples/ or pick an existing OSS Flutter app and document the harness setup against it.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Sample app supports login, settings, and a meaningful task flow
- [ ] Documented setup steps for running ExplorationBinding against it
- [ ] Used as fixture in E5 / E6 / E9 validation

title: Diff compression for repeated screen visits
description: PRD §17 open question: repeated visits to the same screen produce highly redundant diffs. Investigate compression or summarization (e.g. 'route X visited again, no semantic differences') to keep prompts compact in long sessions. Not v1-critical.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Strategy chosen for handling repeat-visit diffs
- [ ] Implemented and toggleable via harness config
- [ ] Measured token savings on a synthetic repeat-visit trajectory

title: CLI trajectory viewer
description: A subcommand (or sibling tool) that reads a JSONL trajectory and renders a readable inline view: per-turn header, action, diff summary, summary update, and final outcome. Useful for human review per PRD §19 success criterion. Lower priority — not on the v1 critical path but explicitly in v1 scope per §16.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Reads a v1 trajectory file and renders all turns inline
- [ ] Highlights validation failures and harness errors
- [ ] Supports filtering by turn range
- [ ] Renders a 30-minute session in a single sitting per PRD §19

value: When user says 'autonomous discovery' or 'autonomous decomp' or asks for decomposition done on my own: do the bulk of exploration and story decomposition without asking clarifying questions. Read the source PRD/design doc, propose seams, create child stories, wire dependencies, and set priorities autonomously. Leave any unresolved design questions as blocking decision beads (type=decision) or as 'human' flags (bd human <id>) on affected stories — do not stall the decomposition waiting for answers. The user reviews the decomposition at the end, not the questions along the way.
